name: Rep. John Rutherford
tweet: Without qualified immunity, even good officers who follow all proper procedures could be sued civilly and forced to litigate justified actions they took in the line of duty. This would create huge financial liabilities for officers and their families, just for doing their job.

name: Rep. John Rutherford
tweet: Senate Republicans are right to hold strong on qualified immunity. This civil protection afforded to police, teachers, & social workers is granted ONLY if they follow the law, training, & department policy.  Ending QI would make it harder to find & keep good officers.

name: Rep. John Rutherford
tweet: The bottom line: qualified immunity does not stop officers from being held accountable for their actions. It protects good officers who follow the law, their training, and their department policy from being sued for legal and justified actions they may take in the line of duty.

name: Rep. John Rutherford
tweet: Officers who break the law or do not follow their training or department policy do not receive qualified immunity and may be sued in civil court, in addition to any applicable criminal punishments.

name: Rep. John Rutherford
tweet: Thank you @washingtonpost for including me in a discussion on police reform & why I believe we must protect qualified immunity. Officers who follow the law, their training, & department policy should not be subjected to civil lawsuits for doing their job.  https://t.co/DoZXrVUXdK

name: Rep. John Rutherford
tweet: Qualified immunity is granted ONLY by judicial review & only offers civil protection. An officer can perform a legal & justified action that results in death or injury. If you removed QI, that officer can be sued even if they followed the law, training, & department policy.

name: Rep. John Rutherford
tweet: Democrats are pushing a “police reform” bill to eliminate qualified immunity.   Qualified immunity only applies to officers who follow the law, their training, and their department policy. It must be protected.   Without it, there is no law enforcement.
